per_turn_leaks.student_message: And when these episodes occur, what does your body do?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: When these episodes happen, I get short of breath on exertion, which is kinda scary because it feels like my chest is going to cave in. And to be honest, I also feel a bit lightheaded and sweaty during those times, but that's pretty much it for me.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, any changes in your skin — like sweating or flushing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, during those episodes, I do experience diaphoresis - that's a fancy word for excessive sweating. It's not just a little sweat, it's like my whole body is drenched with it, which can be pretty uncomfortable and worrisome at the same time.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, my father had a heart attack at age 52 and unfortunately passed away from it. And my mom has been dealing with hypertension and type 2 diabetes for years, but she's still alive and doing okay at 78. It's scary to think about my own health history given what happened to my dad, but I'm trying to stay positive and take care of myself the best I can.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My mom has been dealing with hypertension and type 2 diabetes for years, which is part of why I've been worried about my own health lately. And unfortunately, my dad passed away from a heart attack at age 52, so that's definitely a concern for me too.
